rationale: The pair has clear topical overlap in WOM and timing (the cited paper distinguishes immediate vs. ongoing WOM drivers, while the citing paper studies temporal contiguity effects in WOM evaluation), so it is not safely an obvious incidental citation. However, the available evidence is sparse (only a DOI match, no in-text citation context), and the two papers focus on different outcomes/settings (offline conversation volume vs. online review negativity/usefulness), so importance cannot be confirmed from current data.
